Angela Rayner MP, representing Ashton-under-Lyne (including Audenshaw, Droylsden and Dukinfield) Read more Prev Next STANDING UP, SPEAKING OUT Will you take the next step? The Rt. Hon. Angela Rayner MP and the Labour Party in Ashton-under-Lyne are fighting for a better future. All around our community, people just like you are taking steps to get involved. From doorstep canvassing, to leafleting and attending local events, there’s something for everyone to get involved with. pages.title: ABOUT ME - The Right Honourable Angela Rayner MP
pages.meta_description: Representing Ashton-under-Lyne (including Audenshaw, Droylsden and Dukinfield)
pages.text_excerpt: ABOUT ME - The Right Honourable Angela Rayner MP Skip to main content The Right Honourable Angela Rayner MP Representing Ashton-under-Lyne (including Audenshaw, Droylsden and Dukinfield) Join Get Involved Donate HOME ABOUT ME CONTACT ME NEWS More Search Search the website Search Menu ABOUT ME The Rt. Hon. Angela Rayner MP, representing Ashton-under-Lyne (including Audenshaw, Droylsden and Dukinfield) The Rt. Hon. Angela Rayner is the Member of Parliament for Ashton-under-Lyne (including Audenshaw, Droylsden and Dukinfield) “From the beginning of my working life I’ve always stood up for working people, first as a Trade Union rep representing care workers and then as a regional union official. Now I use the skills I’ve developed to represent the communities of Ashton-under-Lyne.” Angela is not an Oxbridge-educated, former Special Adviser, professional politician. She did not have a privileged upbringing and never went to public school or university. She was brought up on a council estate and left her local comprehensive at 16 with no qualifications and a baby already on the way, after being told she would ‘never amount to anything’. Angela has spoken about how Labour’s Sure Start centres gave her, and her friends, the support they needed to become better parents and to grow and develop as young adults. After a spell at the local FE College, Angela started working as a care worker for Stockport Council where she gained experience at the sharp end of public services. Much of her time was spent providing one-to-one care to elderly people in their own homes, looking after their personal hygiene, preparing meals, listening and showing empathy. She was soon put forward by her women work-mates to speak for them as a union rep with UNISON. “I was mouthy,” she says, “and I would take no messing from management.” She rose through the ranks of the trade union movement with her direct experience of low pay, long hours and zero hours contracts, to become the most senior elected official of UNISON in the North West of England. In 2015, Angela Rayner became the first woman MP in the 180-year history of her Ashton-under-Lyne constituency. She was soon promoted to the shadow whip’s office by the new Labour Leader Jeremy Corbyn and then went on to hold the position of Shadow Pensions Minister, before becoming a member of the Shadow Cabinet as Shadow Secretary of State for Education. In March 2020, Angela was elected as Deputy Leader of the Labour Party. She has also held the roles of Shadow Deputy Prime Minister, Shadow Secretary of State for Levelling Up, Housing and Communities and Shadow Secretary of State for the Future of Work. Following the election of a Labour Government in July 2024, Angela served as Deputy Prime Minister and Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government until September 2025. Away from Parliament and politics, Angela has three sons and a granddaughter. The Right Honourable Angela Rayner MP Contact details Ashton Market Hall, Market Street, Ashton-under-Lyne OL6 7JU Ashton Market Hall, Market Street, Ashton-under-Lyne OL6 7JU '> View on Google Maps Email: angela.rayner.mp@parliament.uk Further details Promoted by Andrew McLaren as Campaign Co-ordinator on behalf of the Ashton-under-Lyne Constituency Labour Party, at 7 Wilson Crescent, Ashton-under-Lyne, OL6 9SA ©2026 Copyright Labour All rights reserved. pages.title: CONTACT ME - The Right Honourable Angela Rayner MP
pages.meta_description: Representing Ashton-under-Lyne (including Audenshaw, Droylsden and Dukinfield)
pages.text_excerpt: CONTACT ME - The Right Honourable Angela Rayner MP Skip to main content The Right Honourable Angela Rayner MP Representing Ashton-under-Lyne (including Audenshaw, Droylsden and Dukinfield) Join Get Involved Donate HOME ABOUT ME CONTACT ME NEWS More Search Search the website Search Menu CONTACT ME The Rt. Hon. Angela Rayner MP, representing Ashton-under-Lyne (including Audenshaw, Droylsden and Dukinfield) If you are a constituent, you can get in touch with me using the form below . Alternatively: WRITE TO ME at Ashton Market Hall, Market Street, Ashton-under-Lyne OL6 7JU CALL ME on 0161 342 5800 EMAIL ME at angela.rayner.mp@parliament.uk Please include your name, phone number and full postal address. Unfortunately, due to strict parliamentary protocol, I am only able to deal with enquiries from constituents of mine in Ashton-under-Lyne, Audenshaw, Droylsden and Dukinfield. You can check if you are a constituent of mine here http://www.theyworkforyou.com/mp/ In order for me to reply to you and undertake any representations on your behalf, I must have: . Your full name . Your full postal address, including post code . Your contact telephone number(s) Please email me again with the above information if you did not include them the first time. Thank you again for getting in touch. CASEWORK My job in Westminster is to speak up for my constituents in the House of Commons, to scrutinise laws and to attend Parliamentary debates and committees. I also have a role locally, supporting local people and community groups, raising and publicising local issues. As your local MP, I can help constituents on a range of issues. Unfortunately, there are some things I cannot do:- I cannot interfere with decisions made by the courts. I cannot help with private disputes. I cannot help with legal matters. I cannot obtain preferential treatment for you or get results outside of the law. Local council There are many issues which should first be taken up directly with the local authority responsible or with your local councillor. You can find your local councillor here: Your Councillors (moderngov.co.uk) If your complaint to the council is not dealt with to your satisfaction, you can contact the Local Government Ombudsman – www.lgo.org.uk Immigration and asylum cases You should first approach the Citizens Advice Bureau: Citizens Advice Tameside – Free, confidential and impartial advice (tamesidecab.org.uk) It is not an MP’s role to offer legal advice. As your local MP, I can chase up the status of your outstanding application if you have not received a response within the given timescale. However, I am unable to alter or influence the process and procedure set out by the Home Office. For more information, please contact the Home Office directly – www.gov.uk/government/organisations/home-office CONTACT FORM FULL NAME * STREET ADDRESS * TOWN * POSTCODE * EMAIL ADDRESS * SUBJECT * MESSAGE * The Right Honourable Angela Rayner MP Contact details Ashton Market Hall, Market Street, Ashton-under-Lyne OL6 7JU Ashton Market Hall, Market Street, Ashton-under-Lyne OL6 7JU '> View on Google Maps Email: angela.rayner.mp@parliament.uk Further details Promoted by Andrew McLaren as Campaign Co-ordinator on behalf of the Ashton-under-Lyne Constituency Labour Party, at 7 Wilson Crescent, Ashton-under-Lyne, OL6 9SA ©2026 Copyright Labour All rights reserved.
